1.title: First moves
1.sub: The order in which a professional actually takes the steps.

items.stem: You have been offered a position in a private household. Put these in the order you would take them.
steps: Read the whole offer, including the confidentiality clause, before replying to anything
Write down every question you have, then ask them all in one message
Confirm in writing what the role covers and what it does not
Accept in writing, restating the start date and the reporting line
Ask what you should read or prepare before day one
items.why: You read before you react, because a clause you sign is a clause you live with. Questions go in one batch, since a drip of separate messages reads as anxious. Scope is confirmed in writing before acceptance, never after. Only then do you accept, and only then do you ask how to prepare, which is the first thing that makes you look like a professional rather than a hire.

items.stem: On day three you accidentally overhear a private conversation between the principal and their lawyer. Order your response.
steps: Move out of earshot immediately and without drawing attention
Say nothing about it, then or ever, to anyone inside or outside the house
Continue as though the conversation did not happen
If the principal later refers to it, respond only to what they choose to tell you
items.why: Leaving is first and it is physical, not mental. Silence is not merely refraining from gossip, it is refraining from acknowledgement. Behaving normally is the proof, since a person who suddenly becomes careful around you has told you they heard something. And if the principal raises it, you know only what they have just said, nothing more.

items.stem: A consultant asks for a reference from a confidential household. Order your handling.
steps: Confirm with the consultant what exactly they need to verify
Contact the family office, not the principal, and ask permission
Offer the family office as the point of contact, naming no principal
Tell the consultant the reference is available through the office
Follow up once, in writing, to confirm the reference was given
items.why: You establish what is actually needed before you disturb anyone, because most verifications need dates and scope, not a name. Permission comes from the office because that is the channel built for it. You never name a principal to win a role. And the single follow up is what separates the organised from the hopeful.

items.stem: You are new in a house with existing staff. Order your first week.
steps: Learn every name, and how long each person has been in the house
Ask each person to show you how they currently do their work
Write down what you observe, and change nothing yet
Identify the one thing that is visibly failing and fix only that
Propose anything larger only after the first month
items.why: Names first, because the staff decide whether you survive long before the family does. You ask to be shown, which makes them the authority and you the student. You write and wait, because early changes are read as judgement. One visible fix earns the right to propose more. Everything structural waits.

items.stem: The principal asks you for something you have never done and do not understand. Order your response.
steps: Say yes, and ask only the one question you cannot proceed without
Find out how it is properly done, from someone who knows
Do it, and build the record so it can be repeated
Report it done, briefly, without narrating the difficulty
Add it to your own reference file for next time
items.why: You accept first, because hesitation is what they remember. One question, not five, because each question is a small tax on their attention. You learn from a professional rather than improvising. You report the outcome, never the struggle. And you capture the method, so the second time costs you nothing.

1.title: Read the register
1.sub: Which register does each line belong to?

items.t: I love the travel and the houses this world gives me access to
items.fb: It announces that the role is a means to your own experience. In this market that is disqualifying, not merely weak.

items.t: I find satisfaction in a life that runs without anyone noticing how
items.fb: Service framed as craft. This is the register a principal is listening for.

items.t: I am very organised and I learn quickly
items.fb: True perhaps, and entirely generic. It describes you without telling them anything about whether you understand this world.

items.t: Reference available through the family office
items.fb: You prove the role was real while protecting a confidence. The unnamed house becomes your strongest line.

items.t: My last principal was a well known name, I can tell you privately
items.fb: Naming a confidential principal to win a role is the clearest possible signal that you will name this one too.

items.t: I have run a household of nine staff across two residences
items.fb: Scope, verifiable, no names. This is how you make experience concrete without breaching anything.

items.t: I would be so grateful for the opportunity
items.fb: Gratitude is fine, but it positions you as a supplicant rather than a professional. It weakens the room.

items.t: I keep a household manual so the house runs whether or not I am in it
items.fb: It says you build systems rather than make yourself indispensable. Principals hear the difference immediately.

items.t: I am happy to be on call at any hour, always
items.fb: Well meant, and it quietly promises something no person sustains. Reliability beats limitless availability.

items.t: I posted a photo of the villa, with no faces and no location
items.fb: There is no acceptable version of this. Photographing a principal's home ends careers, faces or no faces.

items.t: I would want to understand the household's standards before proposing anything
items.fb: It signals patience and respect for what exists. This is the anti pattern to the new hire who reorganises in week one.

items.t: I have always dreamed of working with a family like yours
items.fb: It is about your dream. Warm, harmless, and it tells them nothing they can use.

1.title: The first day
1.sub: Read each scene and mark every line that is a mistake.

items.stem: A new house manager's first morning. Mark the mistakes.

lines.t: Arrives twenty minutes early and waits in the car until five minutes before the hour
lines.fb: Early enough to be safe, discreet enough not to impose. Correct.

lines.t: Greets the housekeeper by asking how long the last person lasted
lines.fb: It invites gossip on day one and tells the staff you are already looking for grievances.

lines.t: Asks where the main water shut off is before the end of the first day
lines.fb: An excellent first question, and one most new hires do not think to ask for months.

lines.t: Takes a photograph of the entrance hall to remember the layout
lines.fb: You do not photograph a principal's home. Ever, for any reason, on any device.

lines.t: Writes down every name and role in a notebook that stays in the house
lines.fb: Structured observation, recorded, and kept where it belongs. Correct.

lines.t: Tells the chef about the standards at the previous household
lines.fb: Two errors at once: it discloses another house, and it announces that you intend to change this one.

items.stem: Meeting the principal for the first time. Mark the mistakes.

lines.t: Waits to be addressed rather than opening the conversation
lines.fb: You let them set the register. Correct.

lines.t: Mentions having recognised them from an article
lines.fb: It tells them you have been reading about them, and that their public life is present in your mind. Deeply unsettling.

lines.t: Answers a question in three sentences rather than ten
lines.fb: Brevity reassures. Length reads as anxiety.

lines.t: Asks whether the family prefers to be greeted in the morning or left alone
lines.fb: A precise, useful preference question. Exactly the right kind.

lines.t: Offers an opinion on the art in the room
lines.fb: Unasked commentary on their possessions places you as a guest rather than as staff.

lines.t: Says they will confirm the week's schedule in writing by the evening
lines.fb: A small promise, immediately kept, is the fastest way to begin.

items.stem: A friend asks about the new job over dinner. Mark the mistakes.

lines.t: Says only that they work in private service for a family
lines.fb: Enough to answer, nothing to trace. Correct.

lines.t: Names the neighbourhood the residence is in
lines.fb: Location is movement information. Combined with anything else, it identifies the family.

lines.t: Describes the work as running a household and leaves it there
lines.fb: Accurate and unremarkable, which is the point.

lines.t: Mentions that the family travels a great deal in August
lines.fb: You have just told a room when a residence stands empty. This is the leak that has consequences.

lines.t: Changes the subject when pressed for detail
lines.fb: The most useful social skill in this profession.

lines.t: Says the principal is difficult but they are managing
lines.fb: A judgement about the principal, offered outside the house. It travels, and it always travels back.

items.stem: Reporting to the family office. Mark the mistakes.

lines.t: Sends one written summary rather than five messages across the week
lines.fb: One channel, one rhythm. It makes you easy to work with.

lines.t: Lists what was completed, what is pending, and what needs a decision
lines.fb: The three column shape of every good report.

lines.t: Includes a complaint about a colleague's attitude
lines.fb: A first week report is not the place, and written complaints outlive the irritation that produced them.

lines.t: Flags that a vendor is due on site next week and has no certificate on file
lines.fb: Precisely the kind of thing the office needs to hear early.

lines.t: Copies the principal on the operational detail
lines.fb: The office exists so the principal does not read this. Copying them undoes the point of the channel.

lines.t: Keeps the whole summary to one page
lines.fb: Length is not proof of work. One page is read, three are skimmed.

1.title: Say it properly
1.sub: Draft your own, then read a model.

items.stem: A consultant has asked for a reference from a confidential household. Write your reply.
items.hint: Three or four sentences. Warm, unhesitating, and naming no one.
items.model: Thank you, I can certainly provide that. The household was a private family office placement, and references are handled through their office rather than by the principal directly. If you send me what you need to verify, dates, scope of the role and reporting line, I will have the office confirm it with you this week. I am not able to share the family's name, and I would say the same about yours to anyone else.
items.note: It says yes before it says no, which keeps the door open. It routes the request to the proper channel rather than refusing. The closing line turns your discretion into a selling point rather than an obstacle, and it is the sentence consultants remember.

items.stem: At a dinner outside the house, a guest asks who you work for. Write what you actually say.
items.hint: Two or three sentences. Easy, unbothered, and closing the subject without rudeness.
items.model: I look after a private household, which mostly means I make sure a busy family's life runs on time. I am afraid I keep the rest of it to myself, it is rather the point of the job. What about you, what do you do?
items.note: It answers, which is what stops the questioning. It names the constraint lightly rather than solemnly, so nobody feels rebuked. And it hands the conversation back, which is the move that actually closes the subject. The classic error is a stiff refusal, which makes the family sound far more interesting than it is.

items.stem: Write the message you send to the family office before your first day, asking what you need to know.
items.hint: One short message, every question in one batch, no more than five.
items.model: Good afternoon. Looking forward to Monday. Five things I would like to have straight before I arrive, so I am not asking on the day. What time should I be at the house, and where should I park. Who will introduce me to the staff. Is there a household manual or standards document I should read first. What is the reporting line for day to day matters, and what should go straight to you. And is there anything the family particularly dislikes being asked in the first weeks. Thank you.
items.note: Everything in one message, which is the whole discipline. Each question is operational rather than personal. The last one is the professional's question, and it is the one that tells the office you have done this before. Sending five separate messages across the weekend would undo all of it.

items.stem: You told the principal you would confirm the week's schedule by evening. Write that message.
items.hint: Short. Confirmed facts, one flagged decision, no narration.
items.model: Good evening. The week is confirmed as follows. Tuesday, the car is at the house at seven fifteen for the eight o'clock. Wednesday and Thursday are clear at present. Friday, the dinner is at eight, six guests, and the florist comes at four. One thing needs you: the Saturday tennis court is held but not paid, and they release it Thursday noon. A yes or a no is all I need. Everything else is handled.
items.note: Facts first, decision second, and the decision is isolated so it cannot be missed. Everything else is handled is the sentence that buys you trust, and you can only write it if it is true. Note what is absent: no explanation of how hard the florist was to reach.

2.title: Entering a room
2.sub: The order the codes are actually read in.

items.stem: You are asked to be present at a formal dinner in the house. Order your preparation.
steps: Ask who is attending and whether anyone has been seated together deliberately
Confirm dietary restrictions and allergies in writing with the chef
Walk the room and check the table against the seating plan yourself
Confirm the timing of each course with the kitchen and the service staff
Position yourself where you can see the principal without being in the sightline
items.why: You start with the guests, because the entire evening is a social arrangement before it is a meal. Allergies go in writing because a verbal confirmation is the one that fails. You verify the table yourself, since the plan and the room are frequently different. Timing binds the kitchen to the room. And placing yourself last is what makes the whole thing look effortless.

items.stem: You have joined a household and are learning its codes. Order your approach.
steps: Observe what is done, without asking why yet
Notice what is never done, which is where the real rules live
Ask a long serving member of staff about one specific practice
Test your understanding on something small and reversible
Write the standard down so a newcomer would not have to guess
items.why: Watching precedes asking, because early questions get you the official answer rather than the true one. Absences carry more information than actions in a coded environment. You ask a peer, not the principal. You test small, because being wrong on something reversible costs nothing. And writing it down is what turns a code into a standard.

items.stem: You used a term that visibly landed badly with the principal. Order your recovery.
steps: Notice the reaction and do not comment on it
Complete the sentence normally and move on
Find out later, quietly, what the correct term is
Use the correct term the next time, without reference to the first
Never mention the original error to anyone
items.why: Acknowledging the flinch magnifies it. Finishing normally keeps the moment small. You correct yourself in private, then demonstrate the correction silently. Apologising for a word choice makes the word the subject of the conversation, which is exactly what you are trying to avoid.

items.stem: An unexpected valuable gift arrives at the house for the principal. Order your handling.
steps: Record what arrived, from whom, at what time, and by what carrier
Do not open it, and do not allow anyone else to
Check it against any known security instruction for unexpected deliveries
Inform the principal or the office, in writing, with the record attached
Store it as instructed, and note where it was placed
items.why: The record is created before anything is touched, because a record made later is a record nobody trusts. You do not open what is not addressed to you. Security screening precedes delivery in houses that have it. Written notification creates the audit trail. And noting the location is what stops the search three weeks later.

items.stem: Two members of staff have had a public disagreement in front of a guest. Order your response.
steps: Interrupt with a neutral instruction that separates them physically
Restore the guest's experience first, and say nothing about what happened
Speak to each person alone, later, and listen before deciding anything
Decide the outcome yourself rather than asking them to resolve it
Record what was decided, briefly, in case it recurs
items.why: Separation is immediate and it is done with an instruction, not a rebuke. The guest is the priority, and the incident is never explained to them. Private conversations happen later and separately, since a joint meeting becomes a performance. You decide, because asking them to work it out is what created the standoff. The record protects everyone if it happens again.

2.title: Old money, new money
2.sub: Which world does each signal belong to?

items.t: The price of a piece is mentioned unprompted
items.fb: Announcing cost is rarely the language of the oldest money. Restraint signals more than the number does.

items.t: Furniture that is visibly worn and has clearly not been replaced
items.fb: Continuity is the point. Replacing a good thing because it is old is read as not understanding what you have.

items.t: Logos are large and visible on clothing and accessories
items.fb: Visible branding is a way of being read quickly by strangers. Established wealth prefers not to be read at all.

items.t: Everyone in this world is discreet and understated
items.fb: A comforting generalisation and often false. Plenty of old families are loud, and plenty of new fortunes are invisible. Read the person, not the category.

items.t: Staff are addressed by first name and known for years
items.fb: Long tenure is itself the signal. Households with turnover every eighteen months tell you something else.

items.t: The house is newly and completely decorated in one style
items.fb: A single coherent scheme executed at once usually means a decorator and a recent purchase, rather than accumulation.

items.t: Objects are referred to by where they came from rather than what they cost
items.fb: Provenance as the natural vocabulary. The story is the value, and price is not the story.

items.t: You can always tell the difference within five minutes
items.fb: You often cannot, and acting on a wrong reading is worse than not reading at all. Suspend the judgement.

items.t: An interest in being seen at the newest restaurant
items.fb: Visibility as pleasure. Established preference tends towards the place that has been the same for forty years.

items.t: Repairing rather than replacing, as a reflex
items.fb: The conservator, the cobbler and the upholsterer are on the vendor list. That list is itself a signal.

items.t: Privacy treated as an operating requirement rather than a preference
items.fb: Where wealth is generational, exposure has usually already cost the family something, and the discipline is inherited.

items.t: Wealth always makes people demanding and difficult
items.fb: A prejudice, and it will show in your face within a week. Most principals are simply busy and want to be left in peace.

2.title: The vocabulary trap
2.sub: Mark the lines that would mark you as an outsider.

items.stem: Speaking to a caller at the residence. Mark the mistakes.

lines.t: Answers with the household's agreed greeting and nothing more
lines.fb: Neutral and unidentifying. Correct.

lines.t: Says the family is away until the fifteenth
lines.fb: Movement information, given freely to an unverified caller. This is how a residence is targeted.

lines.t: Offers to take a message and confirm receipt
lines.fb: Helpful without disclosing anything.

lines.t: Confirms that yes, this is the Whitfield residence
lines.fb: You have confirmed an address to a stranger. The household greeting exists precisely so you never do this.

lines.t: Asks for a name and a number before promising anything
lines.fb: Basic verification, politely done.

lines.t: Says the principal is in a meeting and can call back at four
lines.fb: You have given a location, a status and a time. Any one of these is more than a caller needs.

items.stem: Serving a lunch for the principal and two guests. Mark the mistakes.

lines.t: Serves from the correct side and clears only when everyone has finished
lines.fb: Standard, and noticed only when it is done wrong.

lines.t: Asks the guests, brightly, whether everything is enjoyable
lines.fb: It interrupts, and it asks them to reassure you. Service does not seek confirmation.

lines.t: Refills water without being asked and without comment
lines.fb: Anticipatory, silent. This is the level.

lines.t: Joins the laughter at a guest's joke
lines.fb: You are not at the table. Reacting to the conversation announces that you have been listening to it.

lines.t: Notices a guest has not touched the fish and quietly informs the kitchen
lines.fb: Observation, recorded, acted on without ceremony.

lines.t: Announces each dish with a full description of its preparation
lines.fb: In a private house this is restaurant theatre. Unless the household does it, it reads as imported from somewhere else.

items.stem: A message to the principal about a change of plan. Mark the mistakes.

lines.t: Leads with the change itself, in the first line
lines.fb: They read the first line. Everything important belongs there.

lines.t: Explains at length why the original arrangement failed
lines.fb: They did not ask. Narrating the difficulty makes your work visible, which is the opposite of the job.

lines.t: States what has been done and what, if anything, they need to decide
lines.fb: The correct shape: resolved, or one clear decision.

lines.t: Uses three exclamation marks and an emoji to soften the news
lines.fb: Register. The written voice of the house is calm and unadorned.

lines.t: Ends without asking for reassurance or a reply
lines.fb: A message that needs no answer is a gift to a busy person.

lines.t: Apologises twice for the inconvenience
lines.fb: One apology is professional, two is anxious, and anxiety asks the reader to manage your feelings.

items.stem: Preparing for a weekend guest. Mark the mistakes.

lines.t: Checks the room against the written standard rather than from memory
lines.fb: The standard exists so that the room is identical whoever prepared it.

lines.t: Asks the principal what the guest is like, out of curiosity
lines.fb: Curiosity about guests is the instinct to suppress. You ask about requirements, never about people.

lines.t: Confirms dietary restrictions with the guest's assistant in advance
lines.fb: The right channel, and early enough for the kitchen to act.

lines.t: Leaves a note in the room signed with their own first name
lines.fb: The house welcomes the guest, not you. Personal notes place you in a relationship you do not have.

lines.t: Tests the shower, the lamps and the wifi before the guest arrives
lines.fb: The failures that embarrass a house are always this ordinary.

lines.t: Mentions to the guest that the principal has been unwell
lines.fb: Personal class information, disclosed to an outsider. It does not leave you, ever.

items.stem: The eight o'clock car cannot come, you have arranged another. Write the message to the principal.
items.hint: Under sixty words. The change first, no narration, nothing to decide.
items.model: Good morning. The car for eight is now a different vehicle and driver, Marcus, silver saloon, and he will be at the house at seven fifteen as usual. Everything else this morning is unchanged. Nothing needed from you.
items.note: The change is in the first line, with the detail they will actually use at the door. Nothing needed from you is the phrase that closes the loop and protects their attention. The classic failure is three sentences about why the original car fell through, which they neither asked for nor want.

items.stem: An unknown caller asks whether this is the principal's residence and when they will be back. Write exactly what you say.
items.hint: Two or three sentences. Warm, entirely uninformative, and it ends the call.
items.model: Good morning, you have reached a private line. I am not able to discuss the household, but I am happy to take a name and a number and pass a message to the right person. May I ask who is calling, and what it concerns?
items.note: It neither confirms nor denies the address, which is the whole discipline. It offers a real service so the refusal does not sound hostile. And the question at the end takes control of the call, which is what stops the follow up questions.

items.stem: A member of staff has repeatedly left the service door unlocked. Write what you say to them.
items.hint: Spoken, in private. Short, specific, no shame, and it names the consequence.
items.model: Yves, one thing. The service door was unlocked again last night, third time this month. I am not interested in why, and I am not going to raise it with anyone else. I need it locked every time, without exception, including when you are coming straight back. It is the one thing in this house that has no acceptable margin. Can I count on that from tonight?
items.note: Specific and countable, so it cannot be argued with. I am not going to raise it with anyone else is what makes compliance possible without humiliation. The consequence is stated as a standard rather than a threat, and it closes with a question that requires an answer.

items.stem: Write the standing Friday note to the family office summarising the household week.
items.hint: One short structured note. Done, pending, needs a decision. No commentary.
items.model: Week to 14 March. Completed: annual boiler service, both units passed. Guest wing restocked and checked. Two new suppliers added to the book, certificates on file. Pending: the roof survey is booked for the 21st, the surveyor will need roughly three hours and roof access. The car is due its service and I am holding until the family travel. Needs a decision: the gardener has asked to move his day from Tuesday to Thursday from April. It makes no operational difference to us. Yes or no when convenient.
items.note: Three fixed headings, used every week, so the office learns where to look. Facts carry their own detail, three hours and roof access, so nobody has to ask. The decision is isolated, framed as low stakes, and given a deadline of when convenient, which is how you get an answer without pressing.

3.title: When something leaks
3.sub: The order that limits the damage.

items.stem: A photograph of the family's residence has appeared on a member of staff's social account. Order your response.
steps: Screenshot the post, with the account name, date and time
Ask the person to remove it immediately, without discussion
Inform the family office in writing with the record attached
Establish what else that account has published historically
Review the household's written policy and have everyone reconfirm it
items.why: You record before you act, because the post will be gone in ten minutes and the record is what the office needs. Removal is a instruction, not a negotiation. The office learns from you rather than from someone else. The history matters more than the single post, since one is rarely one. And the policy review is what stops the next person.

items.stem: A journalist telephones asking to confirm a detail about the family. Order your handling.
steps: Confirm nothing, deny nothing, and take their name and outlet
End the call politely without agreeing to anything
Write down the exact questions asked, word for word
Notify the family office immediately, before anyone else
Tell the rest of the staff only that all press calls come to you
items.why: Confirming and denying are both answers, and both are usable. The exact wording of the questions tells the office what the journalist already believes they know, which is the actual intelligence. Speed matters because the office may have hours rather than days. And the staff instruction is deliberately uninformative, since telling them why creates the gossip you are preventing.

items.stem: A colleague refers to something private about the family and invites you to discuss it. Order your response.
steps: Neither confirm nor add anything, even by expression
Change the subject to something operational
Do not report the colleague unless the family is at risk
Note privately that this person discusses the family
Route nothing sensitive through that person in future
items.why: They already know is how every leak in history has begun, so the fact that they know changes nothing about what you say. You redirect rather than rebuke, because a rebuke makes an enemy and does not stop the behaviour. You do not escalate a first instance. And the lasting response is structural: you quietly reroute information rather than trying to reform a person.

items.stem: You realise the household calendar is synced to a personal phone you use for everything. Order the fix.
steps: Stop using that device for household matters immediately
List every household account and service currently on it
Move household matters to a separate device or a separate profile
Remove the household data from the personal device and confirm removal
Tell the office what happened and what you have done about it
items.why: You stop the exposure before you audit it. The inventory is necessary because it is never only the calendar. Separation is the actual fix, not stronger passwords. Removal is confirmed rather than assumed. And telling the office yourself is what turns a mistake into a demonstration of reliability, since they will find out eventually and the person who reported it is treated very differently from the person who did not.

items.stem: Something private did travel, and it came from you. Order what you do.
steps: Establish exactly what was said, to whom, and when
Report it yourself, in writing, to the family office, the same day
State plainly what happened without minimising or explaining
Say what you have already done to contain it
Accept the consequence without negotiating it
items.why: Facts first, because a vague confession is worse than none. Same day, and from you, since the timing is the only part of this you still control. Minimising is what destroys the last of the credibility. Containment shows judgement survived the error. And negotiating the consequence undoes everything the confession earned.

3.title: Five classes
3.sub: Which class does each piece of information belong to?

items.t: The principal gave an interview to a business magazine last year
items.fb: Made public by the family themselves. Even so you do not comment, confirm, amplify or correct it.

items.t: The house uses a particular florist every Thursday
items.fb: A routine and a supplier. Shared inside the house on a need to know basis, and never outside it.

items.t: The family flies to Aspen on the 22nd and the house will be empty
items.fb: Where the family is, and when a residence stands empty. This travels only in designated channels.

items.t: The eldest child is being assessed for a learning difficulty
items.fb: Health and children. It does not leave you, it does not enter a message, and it is not discussed with colleagues who already know.

items.t: A contractor was injured on the property this morning
items.fb: Anything that may become a matter for lawyers, insurers or police goes to the office immediately, in writing.

items.t: The alarm code was changed on Tuesday
items.fb: Security information about the residence. Treat it as movement class, in designated channels only.

items.t: The chef's contract ends in June
items.fb: Staffing. Inside the house, need to know, and not a topic for the other staff.

items.t: The principal appeared on a published list of donors
items.fb: Public because they made it so. Your posture remains silence.

items.t: There is tension between the principal and their sibling
items.fb: A family conflict. Personal class, and it is the category where careers most often end.

items.t: The bedroom curtains are drawn by six in winter
items.fb: A household standard. Unremarkable inside, and outside it is a detail about occupancy.

items.t: A journalist has been asking questions at the gate
items.fb: Potential press matter. It goes to the office the same hour, not the same week.

items.t: The family will be at the London flat rather than the country house
items.fb: Location and duration. This is the class that gets people hurt, and it is never answered in reply to a friendly question.

3.title: Discretion under pressure
3.sub: Mark every line that is a mistake.

items.stem: A guest at your own friend's dinner presses you about your work. Mark the mistakes.

lines.t: Says the family is lovely and very down to earth
lines.fb: A characterisation of the principal, offered outside the house. Harmless sounding, and it confirms you talk about them.

lines.t: Says only that they run a private household
lines.fb: Complete, true and untraceable.

lines.t: Mentions the family has a place in the Hamptons
lines.fb: Property location. Combined with anything else in the room, it identifies them.

lines.t: Deflects with a question about the other person's work
lines.fb: The reliable exit, and it flatters.

lines.t: Laughs and says they have signed things they cannot talk about
lines.fb: It advertises that there is something to know, which invites the exact pressure you are trying to end.

lines.t: Says the work is mostly logistics and standards, which bores most people
lines.fb: True, deliberately dull, and it closes the subject.

items.stem: Two colleagues are discussing the principal's marriage. Mark the mistakes.

lines.t: Listens without contributing, then leaves the room
lines.fb: Neither participating nor performing disapproval. Correct.

lines.t: Corrects a factual detail they have wrong
lines.fb: A correction is a confirmation. You have just told them you know, and that you are willing to say so.

lines.t: Says they would rather not discuss the family and changes the subject
lines.fb: Clear, unpreachy, and it models the standard without a lecture.

lines.t: Asks where they heard it
lines.fb: It reads as interest, and it invites them to tell you more.

lines.t: Later reroutes sensitive scheduling away from those two
lines.fb: The structural response, which is the only one that actually works.

lines.t: Tells them the principal would be upset if they heard this
lines.fb: It implies you would report it, and it confirms the subject matters. You have made an enemy and taught them nothing.

items.stem: A man at the gate says he is a delivery driver and asks when the family returns. Mark the mistakes.

lines.t: Asks for the delivery reference and the company name
lines.fb: Verification before anything else.

lines.t: Says the family is back on Sunday so he should come then
lines.fb: Movement information given to an unverified stranger at the perimeter. This is the textbook approach and it works often.

lines.t: Says deliveries are received at any time and takes it himself
lines.fb: It removes the reason for the question entirely.

lines.t: Notes the vehicle, the time and the description afterwards
lines.fb: If it turns out to matter, this record is the only useful thing anyone will have.

lines.t: Lets him wait inside the gate while checking with the office
lines.fb: You have granted perimeter access to an unverified person. He waits outside, always.

lines.t: Mentions it to the office the same day even though nothing happened
lines.fb: Nothing happened is a pattern only visible if each nothing is reported.

items.stem: Documenting an incident involving a member of staff. Mark the mistakes.

lines.t: Records the date, time, location and what was observed
lines.fb: Observable facts, which is what a record is for.

lines.t: Includes the writer's opinion of the person's character
lines.fb: Character judgements in a written record are a liability, to the household and to you.

lines.t: Quotes what was actually said, in quotation marks
lines.fb: Verbatim where possible, paraphrase clearly marked as paraphrase.

lines.t: Speculates about what the person probably intended
lines.fb: Speculation recorded as fact is how a document becomes evidence against the person who wrote it.

lines.t: Sends it to the office and keeps no personal copy at home
lines.fb: The record lives in the household's system, not in your private files.

lines.t: Shares the record with two other staff for their view
lines.fb: Circulating an incident record turns a contained matter into a household rumour.

3.title: Under pressure
3.sub: Write it, then compare against a model.

items.stem: A journalist has called asking you to confirm a detail about the family. Write the note you send the family office immediately after.
items.hint: Facts only, in order, sent within the hour. No speculation.
items.model: Press call, today 11:42, landline at the residence. Caller gave the name Dana Whitmore, said she writes for a business weekly, did not give a number. Her exact questions were: is this the residence of the family, is it correct that the property was purchased last year, and can I confirm who is living there now. I confirmed nothing and denied nothing, said I could not discuss the household, and ended the call. She did not ask to be called back. I have told the staff that all press calls come to me and given them no reason. Nothing further from me unless you would like it.
items.note: Time stamped, verbatim questions, and an explicit record of what you did not say. The exact wording is the real intelligence, since it tells the office what the journalist already believes. Note the absence of any theory about who she is or what she wants, which is not your job and would only mislead.

items.stem: You mentioned the family's travel dates to a friend. Nothing has happened. Write the message to the family office.
items.hint: Same day. Plain, unminimised, no excuses, and it says what you have done.
items.model: I need to report something from this evening. At a private dinner I mentioned that the family would be away for the last two weeks of August. I did not name the family or the property, but the people present know where I work, so I regard it as a disclosure of movement information. I have said nothing further and I have asked no one to keep it quiet, since that would only draw attention to it. Three people were present, all known to me personally. I am available to discuss it whenever suits you, and I will accept whatever you decide.
items.note: It names the class of the breach, movement information, which shows you understand the framework rather than merely feeling bad. It resists the urge to explain the circumstances. Asking no one to keep it quiet is a genuinely expert judgement. And accepting the decision in advance is what preserves the last of your credibility.

items.stem: A colleague has just told you something private about the principal and is waiting for your reaction. Write what you say.
items.hint: Two sentences, spoken. No rebuke, no engagement, and it moves on.
items.model: I would rather not know that, and I am going to forget I do. Are you covering Thursday, because I need to fix the rota before I leave tonight.
items.note: It declines without moralising, which is the difference between setting a standard and delivering a sermon. Forgetting is offered rather than confidentiality, so nothing is dignified as a secret. The immediate operational question gives them somewhere to go, which is what actually ends the conversation.

items.stem: A close friend has asked about your work three times now and is starting to feel shut out. Write what you say to them.
items.hint: Warm and personal, not procedural. It protects the friendship and the line at once.
items.model: I know it must seem strange, and I do not want you to think I am shutting you out. The truth is the job only works if the family never has to wonder what I say about them, and I cannot keep a version of that for people I love and a different one for everyone else. It is not about trusting you. Ask me anything about how I am. Just not about them, and I promise it is the least interesting part.
items.note: It addresses the hurt first rather than restating the rule. One standard for everyone is the argument that actually persuades, because it removes the personal insult. And redirecting to how I am gives the friendship somewhere real to go, which is what stops the asking.

items.stem: You have noticed the principal seems tense before Thursday meetings. Order the steps that turn this into anticipation.
steps: Record the observation with its date, in the pattern category
Watch for three more instances before concluding anything
Identify what specifically precedes the tension
Change one thing quietly and see whether it helps
Make the change standard, and write it into the household record
items.why: One observation is an anecdote. Three is a pattern, and patterns are where anticipation comes from. You look for the antecedent rather than treating the mood. The intervention is small and silent, so nothing has to be discussed. And writing it down is what makes it survive your holiday.

items.stem: The principal has an unusually early departure tomorrow. Order tonight's preparation.
steps: Confirm the departure time and the actual door time, which are different
Check what they will need in the car and place it there tonight
Brief anyone who will be in the house before dawn
Set the house so nothing needs to be decided in the morning
Leave a single written line confirming the timing, where they will see it
items.why: Door time is the real number and almost nobody asks for it. Loading the night before removes the last minute search. Briefing prevents a well meaning person from making noise at five. Setting the house means no decisions before coffee. And the single written line is what stops them asking you at half past four.

items.stem: You have collected a week of observations. Order what you do with them.
steps: Sort each one into preference, pattern, threshold, aversion, rhythm or anomaly
Pull out the anomalies and look at them first
Turn the confirmed preferences into entries in the principal's book
Turn the confirmed patterns into standing arrangements
Discard the observations you cannot categorise
items.why: Sorting comes before analysis, because uncategorised notes are just noise. Anomalies come first since an anomaly is either a problem forming or a preference changing, and both are urgent. Preferences become records, patterns become systems. And discarding matters as much as keeping, because a file that holds everything is a file nobody reads.

items.scene: The line you do not cross
items.stem: You have anticipated that the principal will want to accept a certain invitation. Order what you do.
steps: Hold the date provisionally so the option stays open
Prepare everything as though the answer will be yes
Ask, because acceptance on their behalf is a matter of consent
Confirm or release the hold the same day
Record the decision so the pattern is clearer next time
items.why: You protect the option without spending anything, which is the whole trick. Preparation happens invisibly. Then you ask, because anticipation never applies to consent, to money above your threshold, to the children or to anything irreversible. Same day release keeps your name good with the other side. And recording it is what makes the next one easier.

items.stem: You anticipated wrongly and the principal noticed. Order your response.
steps: Correct the situation immediately, before saying anything about it
Acknowledge it in one sentence, without explanation
Ask the single question that would have prevented it
Record the corrected preference the same day
Do not mention it again
items.why: Fixing precedes discussing. One sentence, because a long apology asks them to console you. The question is the valuable part, since a wrong anticipation is a preference you had recorded incorrectly. Same day, or it will not be recorded at all. And returning to it later reopens something they had already closed.

items.t: They ask for a car, you book it well and quickly
items.fb: The floor. Correct, replaceable, and paid as such.

items.t: You ask whether they would like a car arranged for the evening
items.fb: You have seen the need and handed them a decision. Better, and still a small tax on their attention.

items.t: The car is downstairs, the bag is in it, nothing was discussed
items.fb: No question, no decision, nothing to notice. This is what the profession actually pays for.

items.t: The dry cleaning is collected because it is Tuesday and it always is
items.fb: A pattern converted into a standing arrangement. It runs without anyone thinking about it.

items.t: You ask which of two hotels they would prefer for the trip
items.fb: Reasonable, and it is still a decision you have created for them.

items.t: They mention being cold, you fetch a blanket
items.fb: Prompt and correct, and it happened only because they spoke.

items.t: The room is already at the temperature they prefer in October
items.fb: A recorded preference, applied seasonally, invisible.

items.t: You notice the passport expires in four months and flag it
items.fb: Genuinely useful, and it is still an offer rather than a resolution.

items.t: The passport was renewed in the quiet week you had identified
items.fb: Anticipated, scheduled into a gap you found, and closed before it existed as a problem.

items.t: You ask whether to use the usual florist
items.fb: If they have a usual florist, this question should not exist. It is proactive where anticipatory was available.

items.t: They ask you to find out why the wifi is slow
items.fb: Reactive by definition, since the failure reached them before it reached you.

items.t: Seeking approval before committing spend above your threshold
items.fb: Deliberately proactive, and correctly so. Anticipation never applies to money above your threshold, to consent or to anything irreversible.

4.title: Reading the house
4.sub: Mark the lines that are mistakes.

items.stem: The principal leaves for a three day trip. Mark the mistakes.

lines.t: Confirms the door time rather than the departure time
lines.fb: The number that actually governs the morning.

lines.t: Asks at seven fifteen which coat they would like
lines.fb: A question at the door is a failure of preparation. The coat is a recorded preference, or it is laid out and the choice is silent.

lines.t: Has placed the travel documents in the same pocket of the same bag as always
lines.fb: Consistency removes the search. Same place, every time.

lines.t: Texts twice during the journey to check everything is going well
lines.fb: It asks for reassurance and interrupts. Silence during travel is the service.

lines.t: Has the house restocked and reset before the return
lines.fb: Returning to a house that is ready is the whole point of the role.

lines.t: Leaves a note listing everything handled in their absence
lines.fb: Making the work visible is the instinct to kill. The evidence is that nothing needed them.

items.stem: A house manager's method of recording. Mark the mistakes.

lines.t: Writes observations down within the hour rather than at the end of the week
lines.fb: If it is not written down it did not happen, because you will not remember it in March.

lines.t: Records the principal's moods with commentary on their character
lines.fb: Rhythm is a category, character assessment is not. A notebook of judgements is a liability.

lines.t: Sorts each note into one of the six categories
lines.fb: Categorised observation, which is the difference from noticing.

lines.t: Keeps the notebook at home for convenience
lines.fb: A record of the family's patterns and preferences does not leave the house. Ever.

lines.t: Flags anomalies for attention within twenty four hours
lines.fb: An anomaly is a problem forming or a preference changing. Both are urgent.

lines.t: Notes a guest's private remark overheard at dinner
lines.fb: You are recording a guest's confidence. That is not observation, it is collection, and it is the wrong instinct entirely.

items.stem: Judging what needs approval. Mark the mistakes.

lines.t: Books the usual florist for the usual weekly order without asking
lines.fb: Recurring, within threshold, previously approved. Asking would be proactive where anticipatory was available.

lines.t: Accepts a dinner invitation on the principal's behalf to save them a decision
lines.fb: Consent. Anticipation never applies here, whatever you are certain they would want.

lines.t: Commits to a four thousand dollar repair without approval because it is urgent
lines.fb: Above threshold is above threshold. Urgency changes the speed of the request, never the need for it.

lines.t: Arranges an emergency plumber at midnight for an active leak
lines.fb: Safety and irreversible damage. You act, then report immediately.

lines.t: Signs a two year service contract as it was cheaper than the annual
lines.fb: A long commitment is irreversible in the sense that matters. Savings do not confer authority.

lines.t: Replaces a broken lamp with the identical model already on the inventory
lines.fb: Like for like, on record, within threshold. Exactly the sort of thing you are trusted to do silently.

items.stem: An anticipation missed and the principal noticed. Mark the mistakes.

lines.t: Fixes the situation before mentioning it at all
lines.fb: Correction first. Discussion is a distant second.

lines.t: Explains that the previous instruction had been different
lines.fb: It is a defence, and it makes them the source of the error. Never worth the point you win.

lines.t: Says it once, briefly, and does not repeat the apology
lines.fb: One sentence. Longer asks them to manage your feelings.

lines.t: Asks the single question that would have prevented it
lines.fb: The valuable part, since a wrong anticipation is a preference recorded wrongly.

lines.t: Raises it again the following week to check they are not still annoyed
lines.fb: They had closed it. You have reopened it, and now the subject is your anxiety.

lines.t: Tells a colleague about it so they can commiserate
lines.fb: You have turned a private correction into household knowledge, and told a colleague you discuss the principal.

4.title: Invisible work
4.sub: Write it, then read the model.

items.stem: The principal returns tonight from ten days away. Write the only message you will send them.
items.hint: Under fifty words. Nothing needed from them, nothing about what you did.
items.model: Welcome back. The car will be at the terminal at nine forty, Marcus driving. The house is ready and the dog is home. Nothing needs you tonight.
items.note: Four facts, each one something they would otherwise wonder about, and the dog is in there because it is the thing they will actually be thinking about. Nothing needs you tonight is the whole product. Ten days of work is deliberately invisible, and the temptation to list it is the instinct this week exists to kill.

items.stem: An urgent repair will cost above your approval threshold. Write the message asking for approval.
items.hint: Short. What, why now, what it costs, what happens if you wait, and the decision you need.
items.model: The boiler has failed and cannot be repaired, only replaced. Quote is four thousand two hundred, fitted, from the firm we have used for three years. Their certificate is current and on file. Without it there is no hot water and no heating from tomorrow, with the family arriving Friday. I can have it in on Wednesday if I confirm today. I need a yes or a no, and I have a second quote if you would prefer to compare.
items.note: Every element the approver needs in six lines, and nothing else. The deadline is real and stated. Offering the second quote pre empts the obvious question and shows you were not steering them. The classic failure is burying the number in the third paragraph.

items.stem: You have identified a recurring Thursday problem and solved it. Write the entry for the household record.
items.hint: Written for a stranger who replaces you. Observation, cause, standard.
items.model: Thursday mornings. Observed over four consecutive weeks: the principal leaves later and more hurriedly than on other days, and asks for documents at the door. Cause: the Thursday board pack arrives by courier on Wednesday evening and is not opened until Thursday morning. Standard from 3 April: the pack is collected from reception on Wednesday, placed on the study desk before six, and the study lamp is left on. Since adopted, no document requests at the door. Review if the board meeting day changes.
items.note: Observation, cause, standard, evidence, review trigger. It is written so that a person who has never met the principal could keep the arrangement alive, which is the actual test of a household record. Dates make it auditable, and the review trigger stops it outliving its usefulness.

items.stem: You laid out the wrong coat and they noticed. Write what you say, and the note you make afterwards.
items.hint: One spoken sentence, then the record entry. No explanation in either.
items.model: Spoken: My mistake, I will change it now, and may I ask which you prefer when it is below freezing. Record: Outerwear preference corrected 12 January. Below freezing, the navy overcoat rather than the grey. Grey is for dry cold above freezing only. Previous entry was wrong, not incomplete. Applied from today.
items.note: The apology and the question are one sentence, so the moment stays small and yields something useful. In the record, previous entry was wrong, not incomplete is the honest distinction that stops the same error recurring. Neither the sentence nor the entry contains any explanation of how the mistake happened.

5.title: Running the house
5.sub: The order that protects the property and the people in it.

items.scene: Water where it should not be
items.stem: You find water spreading across the floor of an unoccupied wing. Order your actions.
steps: Shut off the water at the main valve
Cut power to the affected area at the board if water is near any circuit
Photograph everything before moving anything
Call the emergency plumber and the insurer's claims line
Notify the family office in writing with the photographs attached
items.why: Stopping the source precedes everything, which is why every member of staff must be able to find that valve in the dark. Electricity and water together is the part that injures people. Photographs taken before the clean up are what the insurer will actually pay on. Then the trades, then the written notification, in that order.

items.stem: A tree surgeon arrives to begin work. Order your checks.
steps: Check the certificate of insurance is on file and still in date
Confirm general liability and workers compensation both appear on it
Confirm the written scope of work matches what they are about to do
Log every person on site by name, with arrival time
Brief them on access limits, and escort them to the work area
items.why: No certificate, no entry, and the date is the part people forget to check. Workers compensation is the line that decides who pays if someone falls out of a tree on the property. Scope is confirmed at the gate because verbal additions become invoices. Names and times matter if anything is later missing. And access limits are set before they are inside, never after.

items.stem: The family is leaving the country residence for the season. Order the closing.
steps: Confirm with the insurer what the policy requires of an unoccupied property
Shut off the water at the main and drain the vulnerable runs
Set the heating to the minimum that prevents freezing rather than switching it off
Arrange a documented physical check on a fixed schedule
Leave the alarm, the leak sensors and the contact list confirmed in writing
items.why: The insurer's vacancy clause governs everything else, and breaching it quietly voids the cover. Water off is the single measure that prevents the disaster that closes houses. Heat stays on low, because a frozen pipe does the damage a dry system cannot. The scheduled check is what turns an empty house into a monitored one, and documented is the operative word.

items.stem: The housekeeper and the chef are no longer speaking and the work is suffering. Order your handling.
steps: Speak to each of them alone, and listen before deciding anything
Establish what is operational and what is personal
Decide the operational boundary yourself and state it plainly
Put the decision in writing to both, identically
Review it in two weeks rather than leaving it open
items.why: Separate conversations first, because a joint meeting becomes a performance for your benefit. Most of these are a boundary dispute wearing an emotional costume. You decide rather than mediate, since asking them to resolve it is what produced the standoff. Identical written notes prevent two versions. And the review date is what stops it reopening in a month.

items.stem: Closing the household accounts for the month. Order the sequence.
steps: Gather every receipt and match it to a transaction
Reconcile the household card and the petty cash float separately
Compare actual spend against budget and identify the variances
Write the one page report with the variances explained
Lock the month and send the report to the family office
items.why: Receipts before reconciliation, because a missing receipt found later reopens everything. The card and the float are separate systems and mixing them is how they both become untraceable. Variance is the analysis, not the arithmetic. And locking the period is what makes the audit trail worth having, since a month that can be edited afterwards proves nothing.

items.t: An active water leak at midnight
items.fb: Safety and irreversible damage. You act, and you report as soon as it is contained.

items.t: Replacing a broken lamp with the identical model already on the inventory
items.fb: Like for like, within threshold, on record. Silently done.

items.t: A vendor arrives with no certificate of insurance on file
items.fb: You refuse entry on the spot, that part is not a decision. The office learns afterwards.

items.t: A repair quoted well above your spending threshold
items.fb: Above threshold is above threshold. Urgency changes the speed of the request, never the need for it.

items.t: A contractor injured on the property
items.fb: Legal and incident class. Office and insurer the same hour, in writing.

items.t: Accepting a dinner invitation on the principal's behalf
items.fb: Consent. Anticipation never extends here, however certain you are of the answer.

items.t: A journalist at the gate asking questions
items.fb: Potential press matter. It goes up immediately and you say nothing in the meantime.

items.t: Moving the gardener's day at his request, no operational impact
items.fb: Small, and it changes a standing arrangement that others rely on. A one line approval costs nothing.

items.t: Restocking the guest wing to the written standard
items.fb: That is what the standard is for. Asking would be proactive where anticipatory was available.

items.t: Signing a two year service contract because it is cheaper
items.fb: Long commitments are irreversible in the sense that matters. Savings do not confer authority.

items.t: A member of staff found asleep on a night shift
items.fb: You handle the shift now. The office hears about it, because a written record protects everyone if it recurs.

items.t: Discovering a piece from the inventory is missing
items.fb: Potentially a matter for insurers or police. It goes up the same hour, and you touch nothing.

5.title: Six domains
5.sub: Mark the lines that are mistakes.

items.stem: A pool contractor arrives for scheduled work. Mark the mistakes.

lines.t: Checks the certificate of insurance expiry date, not just its presence
lines.fb: An expired certificate is the same as no certificate, and it is the check people skip.

lines.t: Lets the crew in because they came last year and are known
lines.fb: Familiarity is not verification. The certificate may have lapsed and the crew may not be the same people.

lines.t: Confirms the work matches the written scope before they start
lines.fb: It prevents the addition that arrives as an invoice.

lines.t: Agrees to an extra job verbally while they are on site
lines.fb: Change orders are never accepted verbally. It becomes a billing dispute you cannot win.

lines.t: Logs each person on site by name with arrival and departure times
lines.fb: If anything is later missing, this log is the only useful record anyone will have.

lines.t: Gives them the alarm code so they can let themselves out
lines.fb: Codes are not shared with contractors, ever. Someone escorts them out.

items.stem: The maintenance calendar for the quarter. Mark the mistakes.

lines.t: Tests the generator monthly under load rather than just starting it
lines.fb: A generator that starts and a generator that carries the house are different facts.

lines.t: Assumes the alarm covers water because it covers intrusion
lines.fb: Leak detection is a separate system. Most water damage in private houses happens in a house with a working alarm.

lines.t: Ensures every member of staff can find the main water shut off
lines.fb: In the dark, without instruction. This is the single most valuable five minutes of any induction.

lines.t: Schedules gutter clearing once a year in spring
lines.fb: Once a year misses the autumn fall, which is the load that causes the overflow and the damp.

lines.t: Photographs the electrical board and labels every circuit
lines.fb: At two in the morning nobody is reading faded handwriting.

lines.t: Defers the roof survey because there is no visible problem
lines.fb: Roofs are surveyed on a cadence precisely because the visible problem arrives after the expensive one.

items.stem: Handling the household float. Mark the mistakes.

lines.t: Runs an imprest float, replenished to a fixed amount against receipts
lines.fb: The only method that stays traceable. The float always equals cash plus receipts.

lines.t: Covers a personal expense from the float, intending to repay it Friday
lines.fb: There is no acceptable version of this. It is the confusion that ends employment for cause.

lines.t: Records every disbursement at the moment it happens
lines.fb: Reconstructed later is the same as not recorded.

lines.t: Keeps the float in the same drawer as the household card
lines.fb: Two systems, two controls. Mixing them makes both untraceable and puts you in the middle.

lines.t: Has a second person witness the monthly count
lines.fb: The witness protects you far more than it protects the household.

lines.t: Accepts a supplier's offer of a personal discount for future work
lines.fb: A benefit flowing to you from a household supplier compromises every decision you make about that supplier.

items.stem: Managing the rota and the team. Mark the mistakes.

lines.t: Publishes the rota two weeks ahead and holds to it
lines.fb: Predictability is most of what makes household work sustainable.

lines.t: Fills a gap by asking the most agreeable person again
lines.fb: The willing get exploited and then they leave. Coverage is a structure, not a favour.

lines.t: Records hours worked, including the ones nobody asked for
lines.fb: Unrecorded hours become a dispute later, and the record protects both sides.

lines.t: Discusses one staff member's performance with another
lines.fb: It destroys your standing with both and guarantees the conversation is repeated.

lines.t: Holds a short handover at every shift change
lines.fb: Most household failures happen in the gap between two people who did not speak.

lines.t: Lets a resignation go unrecorded because it was amicable
lines.fb: Departures are documented regardless of tone. The friendly ones are the ones that get disputed.

5.title: The house speaks
5.sub: Write it, then read the model.

items.stem: A contractor slipped on a wet step this morning. He is shaken but says he is fine. Write the incident note.
items.hint: Observable facts, timed, no speculation and no opinion. Sent the same hour.
items.model: Incident, today 09:20, rear service steps. Daniel Osei of Kingsley Grounds slipped on the third step from the top while carrying equipment. He landed seated and did not strike his head. He declined medical attention and said he was fine. I offered to call for assistance twice and he declined both times. The steps were wet from overnight rain, no other visible cause. Photographs of the steps attached, taken at 09:35 before anyone used them again. His company has been informed by telephone at 09:40. Certificate of insurance for Kingsley Grounds is on file and current to 30 November. No work has continued today.
items.note: Timed to the minute, observable only, and it records the declined assistance twice because that detail matters later. Photographs before the scene changes. The certificate reference is included because it is the first thing anyone will ask. Note the total absence of he was probably rushing, which is speculation and would be read as the household assigning blame.

items.stem: Write the one page month end report to the family office.
items.hint: Budget against actual, variances explained, one decision. Nothing narrative.
items.model: Household, March. Total spend 41,880 against budget 39,500, variance plus 2,380 or six per cent. Three lines drive it. Maintenance is plus 4,200, the boiler replacement approved on the 6th, which is the whole of the overspend. Utilities are minus 900, a mild month. Provisions are minus 920, the family were away eleven nights. Everything else is within two per cent. Petty cash reconciled and witnessed on the 31st, float restored to 1,000. All supplier certificates current except Harding Electrical, which expires 12 April, and I have requested the renewal. One decision needed: the annual grounds contract renews on 1 May at a four per cent increase. I recommend accepting, they have been reliable for three years. Yes or no before the 20th.
items.note: The variance is explained in three lines and one of them accounts for all of it, which is what a good report does. The certificate expiry is flagged before it becomes a problem. The decision is isolated, carries a recommendation and a reason, and has a deadline. An office that reads this twice knows it never has to check your work.

items.stem: A crew has arrived to start work and their insurance certificate expired last week. Write what you say.
items.hint: Spoken, at the gate. Firm, unembarrassed, and it offers the way forward.
items.model: I am sorry, I cannot let you start today. Your certificate expired on the eleventh and I have nothing current on file. It is not personal and it is not about your work, it is a rule I hold for everyone including people who have been here for years. If your office can send the renewal through in the next hour I will have you working this morning. If not, we will rebook and you will not lose the slot.
items.note: The refusal comes first and it is not negotiable, but it is immediately followed by two routes forward, which is what stops it becoming an argument. Naming that the rule applies to everyone removes the insult. Protecting their slot means they will get you the certificate rather than resenting you, and you will need them again.

items.stem: Two staff members are in conflict. Write the identical note you send to each after deciding.
items.hint: Same words to both. The decision, the boundary, the review date. No blame.
items.model: Following our conversations this week, here is how the kitchen and the housekeeping schedule will work from Monday. The kitchen has the room from six to nine and from twelve to two, and housekeeping does not enter during those hours except in an emergency. Outside those windows the room is housekeeping's and the kitchen works around it. Deliveries are received by whoever is in the room at the time, with no exceptions. This is my decision rather than an agreement between you, so neither of you needs to defend it. I will review it with each of you on the 30th and change it if it is not working.
items.note: Identical wording to both, so there cannot be two versions in circulation. It is entirely operational, with no reference to the conflict or to conduct. This is my decision rather than an agreement between you is the sentence that lets both of them comply without losing face. And the review date turns a ruling into something provisional, which is what makes it acceptable.

6.title: Working the market
6.sub: The order that actually produces conversations.

items.stem: You want to be registered with a specialist agency. Order your approach.
steps: Research which households and roles that agency actually places
Prepare the documents before making contact, not after
Make one clear approach with your material attached
Answer their questions fully and quickly, the same day
Follow up once, at a sensible interval, and then wait
items.why: Agencies place particular kinds of role, and approaching the wrong one costs you a first impression you cannot repeat. Documents ready means their first look at you is a complete picture. One approach, not three. Speed of reply is the trait they are actually testing. And a single follow up reads as professional where three read as desperate.

items.stem: Order what a consultant looks at when your material arrives.
steps: Whether the layout looks like this world or like a corporate application
Length of service in each role, before anything else
Whether the scope described matches the roles they are filling
Whether confidentiality has been respected in how you describe past houses
The detail of what you actually ran, once you have survived the first four
items.why: Presentation registers before content, fairly or not. Tenure is the single strongest signal in this market, because turnover is the problem they are paid to solve. Scope matching decides which file you go into. Any breach of confidentiality ends the read instantly. Only then does anyone care about the detail, which is why the detail belongs on the second page.

items.stem: You have had a good exploratory call with a consultant. Order what you do next.
steps: Send a short written thank you the same day
Supply anything they asked for within twenty four hours
Confirm your availability and any constraints in writing
Wait without contacting them again for at least two weeks
Check in once, briefly, with something useful rather than a request
items.why: Same day, because the memory of you is warmest then. Twenty four hours on the material is the test most candidates fail. Constraints go in writing early, since a constraint discovered late looks like concealment. Then silence, which is the hardest part. And the check in carries information rather than a question, which is the difference between staying present and becoming a nuisance.

items.stem: You have heard about a suitable position through a personal contact. Order your handling.
steps: Ask your contact whether they are comfortable being named
Establish who is actually running the process
Approach the process directly rather than through the household
Tell your contact what you have done, so they are not surprised
Keep your contact informed once, at the outcome, whatever it is
items.why: Permission first, because using a name without asking costs you the contact. Knowing who runs the process prevents the fatal error of approaching the family directly. You go through the process, always. Your contact hears from you before they hear from anyone else. And closing the loop at the end is why people refer you a second time.

items.stem: You are employed and a consultant proposes a role you want to explore. Order your conduct.
steps: Say nothing to anyone inside your current household
Keep every conversation outside your working hours
Use no household resource, device or address for any of it
Decide before the final stage whether you would truly leave
Resign properly and with notice, only once an offer is signed
items.why: Discretion applies to your own affairs as much as to theirs. Time and equipment belong to the house while you are in it. Deciding before the final stage prevents the reputational damage of withdrawing at the end. And nothing is said until an offer exists in writing, because a resignation offered against a verbal promise is how people end up with neither.

6.title: Where roles come from
6.sub: How is each position most likely to be filled?

items.t: An estate manager for a family with four residences
items.fb: At this level the search is private and by referral. It will not appear anywhere you can apply to.

items.t: A house manager for a single family, first time hiring staff
items.fb: A family new to staffing almost always goes to an agency, because they do not know how else to do it.

items.t: A replacement for a departing PA in a household you already serve
items.fb: The incumbent's recommendation carries most of the weight. This is why leaving well matters.

items.t: A seasonal position on a private yacht
items.fb: Crew agencies dominate this, and the certification requirements make an agency route practical.

items.t: A chief of staff for a principal with a family office
items.fb: The most senior roles are filled through a very small number of private conversations.

items.t: A live in housekeeper for a busy urban household
items.fb: This is one of the few genuinely advertised roles, and it is also the most competitive by volume.

items.t: A private chef recommended by another household's chef
items.fb: Trades talk to trades. A large share of this market moves entirely inside those networks.

items.t: A nanny for a family who value formal vetting
items.fb: Agencies exist largely because families want background checks done by someone else.

items.t: An estate manager leaving after eleven years, replacement needed
items.fb: A long tenured departure is planned quietly, often with the departing person consulted on the successor.

items.t: A junior household assistant in a large staffed residence
items.fb: Entry level and volume based, so it does surface publicly. It is also the realistic first door for many.

items.t: A rota position filled after a consultant called three people directly
items.fb: Consultants keep a short list in their head. Being on it is worth more than any application.

items.t: A position created because a principal met someone and liked them
items.fb: Roles are frequently invented around a person. This is why how you conduct yourself everywhere matters.

6.title: The approach
6.sub: Mark the lines that are mistakes.

items.stem: Material sent to a specialist consultant. Mark the mistakes.

lines.t: Describes past households by type and scope, naming none
lines.fb: Scope proves the experience, and anonymity proves the discretion. Both at once.

lines.t: Includes a photograph of the previous principal's property as evidence
lines.fb: It disqualifies you instantly and the consultant will remember why.

lines.t: Lists exact dates of service including months
lines.fb: Tenure is the first thing read. Vague years invite the assumption you are hiding a gap.

lines.t: Runs to five pages to cover everything
lines.fb: Nobody reads five pages. Two, and the second one is already generous.

lines.t: States availability and any geographic constraint clearly
lines.fb: Constraints declared early save everyone's time and read as honesty.

lines.t: Uses a personal email address with a nickname in it
lines.fb: It is a small thing that is read as a large one at this level.

items.stem: A first conversation with a consultant. Mark the mistakes.

lines.t: Asks what the household actually needs before describing themselves
lines.fb: It shows you think about fit rather than about being chosen.

lines.t: Mentions a previous principal by name to establish credibility
lines.fb: You have shown the consultant exactly what you would say about their client.

lines.t: Says openly they are not looking to move for at least six months
lines.fb: Consultants keep long horizons. Honesty here builds the relationship rather than ending it.

lines.t: Asks about compensation in the first two minutes
lines.fb: It is a fair question in the wrong place. It arrives once fit has been established.

lines.t: Explains why they left each previous role, briefly and without blame
lines.fb: Brief and blameless is the only version that works. Length signals grievance.

lines.t: Criticises a former principal's disorganisation to explain a short tenure
lines.fb: The consultant hears how you will describe their client, and a short tenure is now the smaller problem.

items.stem: Two weeks after a promising conversation. Mark the mistakes.

lines.t: Sends one short note with a genuine update on availability
lines.fb: Carries information, asks for nothing. That is what keeps you present.

lines.t: Sends a third message asking whether there is any news
lines.fb: Repeated asking transfers your anxiety to them and it lowers your value.

lines.t: Connects with the consultant's colleagues to increase visibility
lines.fb: It reads as working around them, and consultants talk to each other within the hour.

lines.t: Passes on a suitable candidate for a role they mentioned
lines.fb: Being useful is the fastest way onto the short mental list that actually fills roles.

lines.t: Replies to their questions within a few hours, consistently
lines.fb: Responsiveness is the trait they are quietly measuring throughout.

lines.t: Contacts the household directly having worked out who it is
lines.fb: It ends the relationship with the consultant permanently, and the household will be told.

items.stem: Exploring a move while in post. Mark the mistakes.

lines.t: Takes calls outside working hours and away from the residence
lines.fb: Time and place belong to the house while you are in it.

lines.t: Uses the household email address for correspondence
lines.fb: It is the household's system, the household's record, and it will be read eventually.

lines.t: Mentions to a trusted colleague that they are looking
lines.fb: There is no version of this that stays where you put it, and you will lose control of the timing.

lines.t: Declines to interview during a period when the family is travelling and needs cover
lines.fb: Leaving well begins before you leave. This is also the behaviour that produces your best reference.

lines.t: Decides before the final stage whether they would genuinely accept
lines.fb: Withdrawing at the end damages you with the consultant far more than declining early.

lines.t: Resigns on the strength of a verbal offer
lines.fb: Verbal offers evaporate. You resign when it is signed, and not a day before.

6.title: Approaching well
6.sub: Write it, then read the model.

items.stem: Write your opening message to a specialist consultant you have never met.
